metadata.title: The Fluency Trap: Polished Output Disarms Verification
metadata.note: CLI video: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(manim) · B05 CHANGE · B06–B08 slates · B09 outro

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.
The more fluently an AI explains something, the less the human checks it. That is automation bias — documented, consequential, and avoidable.
beats.visual_intent: title card: 'Fluency disarms verification — automation bias in five documented cases'

beats.narration_text: Can Claude compile documented cases where polished AI output bypassed human verification with real consequences?

props.command: claude "List 5 documented cases where AI fluency bypassed human verification: domain, year, what went wrong, and how a verification step would have caught it."

beats.narration_text: Five domains, five failures — legal, legal, medical, finance, legal again. Every case had a verification step available. None was taken.

props.code:
# fluency_cases.py  —  Claude Code output
cases = [
  {"domain":"LEGAL",   "year":2023, "error":"Mata v. Avianca: fabricated citations",
   "catch":"Westlaw search"},
  {"domain":"LEGAL",   "year":2024, "error":"Colorado atty: hallucinated precedents",
   "catch":"Pacer/Westlaw"},
  {"domain":"MEDICAL", "year":2020, "error":"IBM Watson: dangerous treatment recs",
   "catch":"Clinical protocol check"},
  {"domain":"FINANCE", "year":2023, "error":"Deloitte AU: hallucinated regulatory refs",
   "catch":"Registry search"},
  {"domain":"LEGAL",   "year":2024, "error":"UK Post Office: errors masked as human",
   "catch":"Audit log review"},
]
for c in cases:
    print(f"{c['domain']} {c['year']}: {c['catch']} would catch it")

beats.narration_text: Five rows, one pattern. In every case, the output was fluent enough to bypass review. In every case, a standard verification step existed and was skipped.
beats.visual_intent: Manim 5-row table: domain, year, error, verification catch — with PASS_CLR highlight sweep on catch column

beats.narration_text: The pattern is structural: fluency correlates with confidence, and confidence correlates with skipping the check. The AI did not cause the failure. The bypass did.

props.command: claude "For each of the 5 cases, estimate: how long would the verification step have taken vs the time spent on remediation after the failure?"

beats.narration_text: Verification time: minutes. Remediation time: months. The asymmetry is not a coincidence — it is the structural signature of automation bias.
beats.visual_intent: slate: verification minutes vs remediation months — 5 case comparison

beats.narration_text: Fluency is not evidence of accuracy. Build the verification step into the workflow before the output is trusted, not after it fails.
beats.visual_intent: recap card: 'Fluency != accuracy. Verify before trust.'

beats.narration_text: Your move: for your next AI output, identify the one verification step that exists and run it before forwarding the result.
beats.visual_intent: next-steps card: 1) identify the verification step that exists, 2) run it before trusting the output

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
